para_trans: Terms and Conditions: -
1) The recognition will remain on a non-subsidised / self-financing basis forever.
2) As prescribed by the Council of Architecture in the present case, as well as the Reign of 21 May 1983
The fulfillment of the conditions in the decision shall be binding on the respective college / institution.
3) All such as may be prescribed by the Government as well as by the Council of Architecture, New Delhi.
Director of Technical Education by the college / institution concerned in respect of acceptance of the terms and conditions.
An agreement will have to be signed with the Directorate, Mumbai. In addition, the necessary documents
and fulfillment of formalities with the consent of the Director, Technical Education.
be satisfactorily completed.
4) Deposits required by the respective college / institution, Director, Technical Education.
Other legal matters must be met by filling out a joint account.
5) The College / Institute is affiliated to Council of Architecture, New Delhi.
With an extension (EOA) for the academic year 2026-27 of the course's accreditation,
Admission will be mandatory for the academic year 2026-27.
6) Admission to this course Admission to the relevant course approved by the Government.
will be done as per the rules / procedure.
7) The fee is higher than the tuition fee prescribed by the fee regulatory authority.
Maharashtra on the college / institution concerned, if charged by the college.
As per the Educational Institutions (Prohibition of Capitation Fee) Act, 1987
Action will be taken.
8) Teachers, non-teaching staff and others to run the course by the respective college / institution.
As per the norms of the Council of Technical Staff Architecture, New Delhi and the respective University.
to be appointed. They should be paid salaries and allowances as per rules. as well as teachers and other
If there are complaints from students to the government regarding the salaries and allowances of the employees, etc.
Strict action will be taken against the college / institution concerned.
9) Books, journals required by the respective college / institution, Council of Architecture
It will be necessary to purchase according to the standard. There is also a library for students.
In terms of usability, the library needs to accommodate learners.
Also, students will have access to all educational facilities (e.g., a school of architecture) as per the Council's standards.
laboratories, libraries, classrooms, etc.) are required to be done.
10) Regarding ragging. The directions of the Supreme Court and the Maharashtra Prohibition of Ragging Act,
Strict implementation of 1999 is binding on the college / institution concerned.
will remain.
11) The immovable required to run the course by the college / institution concerned.
A loan from the government or any financial institution for the property.
The college / institution concerned will be solely responsible for making the repayment.
12) The college / institution must have obtained the affiliation of the respective university.
will remain.
13) Department of Higher and Technical Education, Govt. Registration prescribed under 22.02.2018
If the fee has not been paid by the college / institution concerned, it will be paid by the Joint Director, Technology concerned.
The Departmental Office of Education should make the payment from the respective college / institution.

The other terms and conditions provided shall be binding on the institution / college.
